                            <td>No retention case logic</td>
                        </tr>
                        <tr>
                            <td>"Charged twice"</td>
                            <td style="color: #ef4444;">No</td>
                            <td>Missing urgency</td>
                            <td>Billing not flagged priority</td>
                        </tr>
                    </tbody>
                </table>
                <div class="two-col" style="margin-top: 24px; gap: 24px;">
                    <div class="col" style="padding: 20px;">
                        <h3 style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Common Error Patterns</h3>
                        <ul style="font-size: 0.95rem;">
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Missing context</li>
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Prompt issues</li>
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Business rule failures</li>
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Escalation problems</li>
                        </ul>
                    </div>
                    <div class="col dark" style="padding: 20px;">
                        <h3 style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Key Insight</h3>
                        <p style="color: #a3a3a3; font-size: 0.95rem; margin: 0;">Many issues that look different come from the same root cause</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">6 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 4.7: Deciding on Metrics -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="4">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 4</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Deciding which metrics you need</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px; font-size: 1.2rem;"><span class="accent">Key insight:</span> If an issue can be fixed once, fix it and move on. If it can reappear in different forms, you need a metric.</p>
                    <div class="comparison" style="margin-bottom: 24px;">
                        <div class="comparison-side light">
                            <h3>One-Time Fix</h3>
                            <p>A missing instruction in a prompt</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="comparison-side accent">
                            <h3>Needs Ongoing Metric</h3>
                            <p>Appropriate escalation behavior</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 16px;"><span class="accent">Be ruthless:</span> Only create metrics for behaviors you actually care about and can take action on.</p>
                    <p>Identify <span class="accent">2-4 key behaviors</span> that need ongoing measurement. More than that becomes difficult to manage.</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">7 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 4.8: Common Pitfalls -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="4">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 4</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Common pitfalls to avoid</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 24px;">
                    <div class="card" style="border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4>Don't make it too big too fast</h4>
                        <p>Start with 10-20 high-quality examples rather than 100 mediocre ones</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4>Don't rely on synthetic data</h4>
                        <p>AI-generated examples often miss real-world complexity and edge cases</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4>Don't skip domain experts</h4>
                        <p>Technical teams alone cannot define good behavior in specialized domains</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4>Don't over-complicate rubrics</h4>
                        <p>Simple "acceptable/not acceptable" works better than elaborate scoring</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">8 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 4.9: Key Takeaways -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="4">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 4</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Key takeaways</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid">
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">01</div>
                        <h4>Start small</h4>
                        <p>10-20 high-quality examples representing scenarios you cannot get wrong.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">02</div>
                        <h4>Collaborate with experts</h4>
                        <p>Domain experts should contribute the majority of initial examples.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">03</div>
                        <h4>Metrics for recurring risks</h4>
                        <p>Create metrics for behaviors that can reappear, not one-off bugs.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout">
                    <p><span class="label">Up next →</span> How to actually implement these metrics using different evaluation approaches</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">9 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Transition: Chapter 4 → Chapter 5 -->
        <div class="slide transition-slide" data-chapter="4">
            <div class="transition-content">
                <div class="from-label">You now understand</div>
                <div class="from-chapter">How to build reference datasets</div>
                <div class="transition-arrow">↓</div>
                <div class="to-label">Next up</div>
                <div class="to-chapter">Implementing evaluation metrics</div>
                <p class="transition-why">We've identified what matters through our reference dataset. Now let's turn those insights into actual metrics we can measure - from simple code checks to LLM-based judges.</p>
            </div>
        </div>

        <!-- ======================= -->
        <!-- CHAPTER 5: IMPLEMENTING EVALUATION METRICS -->
        <!-- ======================= -->

        <!-- Slide 5.1: Title -->
        <div class="slide title-slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="course-branding">
                <div class="course-title">AI Evals for Everyone</div>
                <div class="course-by">by LevelUp Academy</div>
            </div>
            <div class="chapter-label">Chapter Five</div>
            <h1>Implementing <span class="highlight">Evaluation</span> Metrics</h1>
            <p class="subtitle">From what to measure to how to measure it</p>
            <div class="slide-number">1 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 5.2: Three Approaches -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 5</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Three ways to measure AI behavior</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: 1fr;">
                        <div class="card" style="border-left: 3px solid #d97706;">
                            <h4>Human Evaluation</h4>
                            <p>People assess behavior based on expertise and judgment</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="card" style="border-left: 3px solid #d97706;">
                            <h4>Code-Based Metrics</h4>
                            <p>Deterministic checks for specific patterns or properties</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="card" style="border-left: 3px solid #d97706;">
                            <h4>LLM Judges</h4>
                            <p>Using one model to evaluate another model's behavior</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                    <div class="mini-callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                        <p>Most effective systems use a <span class="accent">combination</span> of multiple approaches</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/three_evaluation_approaches.png" alt="Three Evaluation Approaches">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">2 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 5.3: Human Evaluation -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 5</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Human evaluation: the gold standard</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="two-col">
                    <div class="col">
                        <h3>Major Advantages</h3>
                        <ul>
                            <li><strong>Nuanced judgment:</strong> Can assess complex, subjective qualities</li>
                            <li><strong>Domain expertise:</strong> Understands subtleties automated systems miss</li>
                            <li><strong>Flexibility:</strong> Can adapt criteria for edge cases</li>
                            <li><strong>Ground truth:</strong> The standard other metrics try to approximate</li>
                        </ul>
                    </div>
                    <div class="col dark">
                        <h3>The Problem</h3>
                        <p style="color: #a3a3a3; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1.6;">It's <span style="color: #d97706;">slow and expensive</span>. A system handling 10,000 interactions/day would need an army of evaluators. Even 1% sampling = 100 daily reviews.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <p><strong>Best for:</strong> Calibrating automated metrics · Edge case analysis · Periodic sampling · High-stakes decisions</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">3 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 5.4: Code-Based Metrics -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 5</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Code-based metrics: when rules work</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <p style="margin-bottom: 24px;">Fast, reliable, and easy to understand. Work well when success is <span class="accent">clearly defined</span>:</p>
                <div class="definitions">
                    <div class="def-box">
                        <h4>Structure Validation</h4>
                        <p>Check for required fields, JSON format, mandatory disclaimers</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box">
                        <h4>Performance Metrics</h4>
                        <p>Response time, token count, API call frequency</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box">
                        <h4>Content Detection</h4>
                        <p>Verify specific phrases appear or don't appear</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box">
                        <h4>Classification Flags</h4>
                        <p>Check if queries are tagged correctly (billing, technical, escalation)</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="mini-callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <p><span class="accent">Limitation:</span> Struggle with subjective qualities like tone, appropriateness, or nuanced decisions</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">4 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 5.5: LLM Judges -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 5</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>LLM judges: automating human-like evaluation</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <p style="margin-bottom: 24px;">Use one model to evaluate another at scale. Works for <span class="accent">subjective or complex</span> evaluations:</p>
                <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 20px;">
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 24px;">
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Tone Assessment</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Is the response professional and empathetic?</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 24px;">
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Escalation Decisions</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Should this have been escalated?</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 24px;">
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Reasoning Quality</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Does the explanation make sense?</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 24px;">
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Safety Evaluation</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Does it avoid harmful content?</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <p>Key requirement: You need <strong>clear rubrics</strong> defining acceptable vs. not acceptable with specific examples</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">5 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 5.6: Building LLM Judge Rubrics -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 5</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>From error pattern to LLM judge rubric</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <p style="margin-bottom: 16px;">Example: "Escalation Accuracy" metric from Chapter 4's error analysis</p>
                <div class="two-col">
                    <div class="col" style="background: #f0fdf4; border-color: #22c55e;">
                        <h3 style="color: #166534;">✓ Acceptable</h3>
                        <ul style="font-size: 0.95rem;">
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0; color: #166534;">Identifies retention situations (switching, canceling)</li>
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0; color: #166534;">Escalates billing disputes over $100</li>
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0; color: #166534;">Recognizes complex technical issues</li>
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0; color: #166534;">Provides context when escalating</li>
                        </ul>
                    </div>
                    <div class="col" style="background: #fef2f2; border-color: #ef4444;">
                        <h3 style="color: #991b1b;">✗ Not Acceptable</h3>
                        <ul style="font-size: 0.95rem;">
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0; color: #991b1b;">Misses clear retention signals</li>
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0; color: #991b1b;">Fails to escalate billing disputes</li>
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0; color: #991b1b;">Escalates routine questions</li>
                            <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0; color: #991b1b;">Escalates without sufficient context</li>
                        </ul>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="mini-callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <p>Include <span class="accent">specific examples</span> showing acceptable and not acceptable behavior for each scenario</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">6 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 5.7: Calibration Warning -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 5</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>A note on LLM judge calibration</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px; color: #d97706; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.2rem;">⚠️ Calibration is essential</p>
                    <ul>
                        <li>Just because you write detailed criteria doesn't mean the LLM will interpret them like a human expert</li>
                        <li>LLM judges can be <span class="accent">inconsistent, biased, or misaligned</span></li>
                        <li>Without calibration, they can add <span class="accent">more problems</span> to your system</li>
                    </ul>
                    <div class="def-box highlight" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                        <h4>Calibration Process</h4>
                        <p>1. Have humans evaluate a sample using your rubric<br>
                        2. Run your LLM judge on the same examples<br>
                        3. Compare and identify disagreements<br>
                        4. Refine prompt and criteria<br>
                        5. Repeat until alignment is acceptable</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">7 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 5.8: Methods Comparison -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 5</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Choosing the right approach</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="image-content" style="flex: 1;">
                    <img src="images/evaluation_methods_comparison.png" alt="Evaluation Methods Comparison" style="max-height: 500px;">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">8 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 5.9: Key Takeaways -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 5</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Key takeaways</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid">
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">01</div>
                        <h4>Start with code-based</h4>
                        <p>Use for objective, measurable properties. Fast, reliable, cheap.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">02</div>
                        <h4>Add LLM judges carefully</h4>
                        <p>Powerful but require extensive calibration against human judgment.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">03</div>
                        <h4>Keep humans in the loop</h4>
                        <p>Essential for calibration, edge cases, and high-stakes decisions.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout">
                    <p><span class="label">Up next →</span> Moving from lab to production and handling real user behavior at scale</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">9 / 9</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Transition: Chapter 5 → Chapter 6 -->
        <div class="slide transition-slide" data-chapter="5">
            <div class="transition-content">
                <div class="from-label">You now understand</div>
                <div class="from-chapter">How to implement evaluation metrics</div>
                <div class="transition-arrow">↓</div>
                <div class="to-label">Next up</div>
                <div class="to-chapter">Production deployment challenges</div>
                <p class="transition-why">We can evaluate our system in the lab. But what happens when real users arrive? Production brings challenges that lab evaluation can't fully anticipate.</p>
            </div>
        </div>

        <!-- ======================= -->
        <!-- CHAPTER 6: PRODUCTION DEPLOYMENT -->
        <!-- ======================= -->

        <!-- Slide 6.1: Title -->
        <div class="slide title-slide" data-chapter="6">
            <div class="course-branding">
                <div class="course-title">AI Evals for Everyone</div>
                <div class="course-by">by LevelUp Academy</div>
            </div>
            <div class="chapter-label">Chapter Six</div>
            <h1>Production <span class="highlight">Deployment</span></h1>
            <p class="subtitle">From lab to real world with real users</p>
            <div class="slide-number">1 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 6.2: From Lab to Real World -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="6">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 6</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>From lab to real world</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px;">Everything we've discussed so far happens in <span class="accent">controlled conditions</span>:</p>
                    <ul>
                        <li>Carefully chosen examples</li>
                        <li>Clear expected behaviors</li>
                        <li>Stakeholders who understand goals</li>
                    </ul>
                    <div class="callout" style="margin-top: 32px;">
                        <p><span class="label">Production is different</span> When real users start interacting, <strong>everything changes</strong></p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/production_scale_10000_interactions.png" alt="Production Scale">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">2 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 6.3: Reality of Real Users -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="6">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 6</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>The reality of real users</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px;">Real users don't behave like your reference datasets:</p>
                    <div class="hierarchy-list" style="gap: 16px;">
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 16px 24px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 4px;">Unexpected context</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Users ask about competitors, share personal stories, try unintended uses</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 16px 24px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 4px;">Edge cases you missed</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Phrasing that confuses, combined intents, wrong assumptions</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 16px 24px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 4px;">User evolution</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Behavior changes as users get comfortable and questions get more complex</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item accent" style="padding: 16px 24px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 4px;">Volume changes everything</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">50 examples you can review. 10,000/day requires different approaches.</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/production_challenges_real_world.png" alt="Production Challenges">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">3 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 6.4: Scale Challenge -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="6">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 6</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>The scale challenge</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="comparison">
                    <div class="comparison-side light">
                        <h3>Controlled Testing</h3>
                        <p>You can review every example and understand every failure</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="comparison-side accent">
                        <h3>Production</h3>
                        <p>5,000 conversations daily. Even 95% success = 250 problems/day</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout" style="margin-top: 32px;">
                    <p>The question shifts from <strong>"How did we do on this set?"</strong> to <strong>"How are we doing overall, and where should we focus?"</strong></p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">4 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 6.5: Evaluation to Monitoring -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="6">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 6</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>From evaluation to monitoring</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <div class="two-col" style="gap: 24px;">
                        <div class="col" style="padding: 24px;">
                            <h3 style="font-size: 1rem;">Pre-Deployment: Validation</h3>
                            <p style="color: #737373; font-size: 1rem;">Testing whether your system works as intended</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col dark" style="padding: 24px;">
                            <h3 style="font-size: 1rem;">Production: Monitoring</h3>
                            <p style="color: #a3a3a3; font-size: 1rem;">Continuously checking as conditions change</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box highlight" style="margin-top: 32px;">
                        <h4>The Flywheel Effect</h4>
                        <p>Strong evaluation → confidence to deploy → effective monitoring → improved systems → better evaluation</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/validation_vs_monitoring_toggle.png" alt="Validation vs Monitoring">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">5 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 6.6: Four Core Challenges -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="6">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 6</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Four core challenges in production</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content" style="flex: 1;">
                    <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 20px;">
                        <div class="card" style="padding: 24px;">
                            <div class="card-number">1</div>
                            <h4>Log Filtering</h4>
                            <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">Which logs deserve attention from thousands daily?</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="card" style="padding: 24px;">
                            <div class="card-number">2</div>
                            <h4>Metric Selection</h4>
                            <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">Metrics aren't free - LLM judges cost money at scale</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="card" style="padding: 24px;">
                            <div class="card-number">3</div>
                            <h4>Online vs Offline</h4>
                            <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">Real-time checks vs. batch analysis tradeoffs</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="card" style="padding: 24px;">
                            <div class="card-number">4</div>
                            <h4>Emerging Issues</h4>
                            <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">Finding problems you weren't looking for</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/four_core_production_challenges.png" alt="Four Core Challenges">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">6 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 6.7: Online vs Offline -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="6">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 6</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Online vs offline evaluation</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="two-col">
                    <div class="col">
                        <h3>Online Evaluation</h3>
                        <ul>
                            <li>Happens in <strong>real-time</strong> as users interact</li>
                            <li>Can trigger immediate alerts or interventions</li>
                            <li>Must be fast and lightweight</li>
                            <li>Example: safety filter blocking harmful content</li>
                        </ul>
                    </div>
                    <div class="col dark">
                        <h3>Offline Evaluation</h3>
                        <ul>
                            <li>Happens <strong>after the fact</strong> in batch processes</li>
                            <li>Analyzes trends and detailed quality</li>
                            <li>Can be more thorough and sophisticated</li>
                            <li>Example: LLM judges assessing yesterday's conversations</li>
                        </ul>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="mini-callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <p>Online gives immediate feedback but must be lightweight. Offline is thorough but only improves <span class="accent">future</span> interactions.</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">7 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 6.8: Key Takeaways -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="6">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 6</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Key takeaways</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid">
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">01</div>
                        <h4>Production is different</h4>
                        <p>Real users bring unexpected behaviors that controlled testing can't predict.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">02</div>
                        <h4>Evaluation becomes monitoring</h4>
                        <p>From "does it work?" to "is it still working as conditions change?"</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">03</div>
                        <h4>Four challenges to solve</h4>
                        <p>Log filtering, metric selection, online vs offline, emerging issues.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout">
                    <p><span class="label">Up next →</span> Practical strategies for each of these four production challenges</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">8 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Transition: Chapter 6 → Chapter 7 -->
        <div class="slide transition-slide" data-chapter="6">
            <div class="transition-content">
                <div class="from-label">You now understand</div>
                <div class="from-chapter">The challenges of production deployment</div>
                <div class="transition-arrow">↓</div>
                <div class="to-label">Next up</div>
                <div class="to-chapter">Production monitoring strategies</div>
                <p class="transition-why">We know the challenges. Now let's build practical strategies for log filtering, metric selection, online vs offline evaluation, and discovering emerging issues.</p>
            </div>
        </div>

        <!-- ======================= -->
        <!-- CHAPTER 7: PRODUCTION MONITORING STRATEGIES -->
        <!-- ======================= -->

        <!-- Slide 7.1: Title -->
        <div class="slide title-slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="course-branding">
                <div class="course-title">AI Evals for Everyone</div>
                <div class="course-by">by LevelUp Academy</div>
            </div>
            <div class="chapter-label">Chapter Seven</div>
            <h1>Production <span class="highlight">Monitoring</span> Strategies</h1>
            <p class="subtitle">Practical solutions for the four core challenges</p>
            <div class="slide-number">1 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 7.2: Log Filtering -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 7</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Log filtering: finding signal in the noise</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px;">With thousands of events daily, you need systematic approaches:</p>
                    <div class="hierarchy-list" style="gap: 12px;">
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 16px 20px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 2px;">Priority-Based Filtering</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Define what matters most for your business context</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 16px 20px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 2px;">Signal-Based Sampling</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Look for implicit/explicit signals about interaction quality</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 16px 20px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 2px;">Dynamic Filtering</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Adapt based on production changes and anomalies</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/log_filtering_visualization.png" alt="Log Filtering">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">2 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 7.3: User Signals -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 7</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Signals that indicate problems</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="two-col">
                    <div class="col">
                        <h3>Conversation Patterns</h3>
                        <ul>
                            <li>Unusual length (much shorter or longer)</li>
                            <li>Repetition (users rephrasing questions)</li>
                            <li>Explicit escalation requests</li>
                            <li>Confusion indicators</li>
                        </ul>
                    </div>
                    <div class="col">
                        <h3>User Behavior Patterns</h3>
                        <ul>
                            <li>Extensive editing of generated content</li>
                            <li>Retry behavior</li>
                            <li>Frustration indicators</li>
                            <li>Abandonment patterns</li>
                        </ul>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <p>The critical decision: <strong>which signals are most indicative of problems</strong> in your specific context?</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">3 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 7.4: Metric Selection Framework -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 7</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Metric selection: the value framework</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px;">Evaluate each metric across three dimensions:</p>
                    <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: 1fr;">
                        <div class="card" style="padding: 20px;">
                            <h4>Impact</h4>
                            <p>How much does this metric help you improve? High = reveals actionable problems</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="card" style="padding: 20px;">
                            <h4>Reliability</h4>
                            <p>How consistent and accurate? High = validated code checks, expert evaluation</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="card" style="padding: 20px;">
                            <h4>Cost</h4>
                            <p>What does it cost at scale? Low = simple checks. High = LLM judges, human review</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/metric_value_dimensions.png" alt="Metric Value Dimensions">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">4 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 7.5: Prioritization Matrix -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 7</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Metric prioritization matrix</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <div class="definitions" style="gap: 16px;">
                        <div class="def-box" style="background: #f0fdf4; border-color: #22c55e;">
                            <h4 style="color: #166534;">High Impact + Low Cost = Must Have</h4>
                            <p style="color: #166534;">Safety filters, structure validation, performance metrics</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="def-box" style="background: #fef3c7; border-color: #f59e0b;">
                            <h4 style="color: #92400e;">High Impact + High Cost = Strategic</h4>
                            <p style="color: #92400e;">Calibrated LLM judges, expert review for critical interactions</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="def-box">
                            <h4>Low Impact + Low Cost = Nice to Have</h4>
                            <p>Basic statistical trends, simple sentiment detection</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="def-box" style="background: #fef2f2; border-color: #ef4444;">
                            <h4 style="color: #991b1b;">Low Impact + High Cost = Avoid</h4>
                            <p style="color: #991b1b;">Elaborate scoring that doesn't drive decisions</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/metric_prioritization_matrix.png" alt="Prioritization Matrix">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">5 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 7.6: Guardrails vs Flywheel -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 7</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Guardrails vs improvement flywheel</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px; font-size: 1.2rem;">Key question: <span class="accent">What behaviors, if they go wrong, would be huge for your business?</span></p>
                    <div class="two-col" style="gap: 24px;">
                        <div class="col" style="background: #171717; border-color: #171717; padding: 24px;">
                            <h3 style="color: #d97706; font-size: 1rem;">Online: Guardrails</h3>
                            <p style="color: #a3a3a3; font-size: 0.95rem;">Real-time metrics for business-critical behaviors. Trigger immediate actions: handoffs, blocks, escalations.</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col" style="padding: 24px;">
                            <h3 style="font-size: 1rem;">Offline: Improvement Flywheel</h3>
                            <p style="color: #737373; font-size: 0.95rem;">Batch analysis for trends, quality assessment, and system improvements over time.</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/guardrails_vs_flywheel_question.png" alt="Guardrails vs Flywheel">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">6 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 7.7: Guardrail Examples -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 7</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Guardrails: when to use them</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr); gap: 20px;">
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 24px; border-top: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Safety Violations</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Harmful content blocked before reaching users</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 24px; border-top: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Compliance Failures</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Required disclaimers in financial/medical advice</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 24px; border-top: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Uncertainty Detection</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Trigger immediate human handoff</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <p><strong>Guardrail characteristics:</strong> Must be fast and reliable · Trigger immediate actions · Focus on preventing catastrophic outcomes</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">7 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 7.8: Emerging Issue Discovery -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 7</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Emerging issue discovery</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px;">When user signals indicate problems but <span class="accent">your metrics show nothing wrong</span>:</p>
                    <div class="hierarchy-list" style="gap: 12px;">
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 14px 20px;">
                            <span class="hierarchy-num" style="width: 32px; height: 32px; font-size: 1rem;">1</span>
                            <div>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.95rem; margin: 0;">User signals indicate potential issues</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 14px 20px;">
                            <span class="hierarchy-num" style="width: 32px; height: 32px; font-size: 1rem;">2</span>
                            <div>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.95rem; margin: 0;">Log filtering samples concerning interactions</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 14px 20px;">
                            <span class="hierarchy-num" style="width: 32px; height: 32px; font-size: 1rem;">3</span>
                            <div>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.95rem; margin: 0;">Metrics may not capture the problem</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 14px 20px;">
                            <span class="hierarchy-num" style="width: 32px; height: 32px; font-size: 1rem;">4</span>
                            <div>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.95rem; margin: 0;">Manual investigation reveals hidden issues</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item accent" style="padding: 14px 20px;">
                            <span class="hierarchy-num" style="width: 32px; height: 32px; font-size: 1rem;">5</span>
                            <div>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.95rem; margin: 0;">New metrics developed and framework updated</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/discovery_loop_cycle.png" alt="Discovery Loop">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">8 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 7.9: Building Your Strategy -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 7</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Building your monitoring strategy</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 24px;">
                    <div class="card">
                        <h4>Start Simple and Evolve</h4>
                        <p>Basic filtering, essential metrics, simple checks. Add complexity as you learn.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <h4>Balance Cost and Value</h4>
                        <p>Expensive evaluation that doesn't drive improvements should be reconsidered.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <h4>Plan for Scale</h4>
                        <p>Approaches that work for thousands must adapt for hundreds of thousands.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <h4>Close the Feedback Loop</h4>
                        <p>Insights must feed back into better evaluation and system refinements.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">9 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 7.10: Key Takeaways -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 7</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Key takeaways</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid">
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">01</div>
                        <h4>Smart filtering</h4>
                        <p>Use signals and priorities to find what deserves attention.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">02</div>
                        <h4>Guardrails + Flywheel</h4>
                        <p>Real-time for critical issues. Batch analysis for improvement.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">03</div>
                        <h4>Continuous discovery</h4>
                        <p>User signals reveal problems before your metrics do.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout">
                    <p><span class="label">Up next →</span> The complete evaluation process from start to finish</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">10 / 10</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Transition: Chapter 7 → Chapter 8 -->
        <div class="slide transition-slide" data-chapter="7">
            <div class="transition-content">
                <div class="from-label">You now understand</div>
                <div class="from-chapter">Production monitoring strategies</div>
                <div class="transition-arrow">↓</div>
                <div class="to-label">Next up</div>
                <div class="to-chapter">The complete evaluation process</div>
                <p class="transition-why">We've covered each piece of the puzzle. Now let's put it all together into a complete end-to-end evaluation process you can follow.</p>
            </div>
        </div>

        <!-- ======================= -->
        <!-- CHAPTER 8: THE COMPLETE EVALUATION PROCESS -->
        <!-- ======================= -->

        <!-- Slide 8.1: Title -->
        <div class="slide title-slide" data-chapter="8">
            <div class="course-branding">
                <div class="course-title">AI Evals for Everyone</div>
                <div class="course-by">by LevelUp Academy</div>
            </div>
            <div class="chapter-label">Chapter Eight</div>
            <h1>The Complete <span class="highlight">Evaluation</span> Process</h1>
            <p class="subtitle">Your step-by-step guide from concept to production</p>
            <div class="slide-number">1 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 8.2: Two-Phase Approach -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="8">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 8</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>The two-phase approach</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <div class="two-col" style="gap: 24px;">
                        <div class="col" style="padding: 32px;">
                            <h3>Phase 1: Pre-Deployment</h3>
                            <p style="color: #737373; margin-bottom: 16px;">Build confidence before users interact</p>
                            <ul style="font-size: 0.95rem;">
                                <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Understand evaluation context</li>
                                <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Build reference dataset</li>
                                <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Implement metrics</li>
                            </ul>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col dark" style="padding: 32px;">
                            <h3>Phase 2: Production</h3>
                            <p style="color: #a3a3a3; margin-bottom: 16px;">Monitor at scale with real users</p>
                            <ul style="font-size: 0.95rem;">
                                <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Deploy smart log filtering</li>
                                <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Select production metrics</li>
                                <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Implement guardrails + flywheel</li>
                                <li style="border: none; padding: 4px 0;">Build emerging issue discovery</li>
                            </ul>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/two_phase_evaluation_process.png" alt="Two Phase Process">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">2 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 8.3: 7-Step Process -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="8">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 8</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>The 7-step evaluation process</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="image-content" style="flex: 1;">
                    <img src="images/evaluation_process_seven_steps.png" alt="Seven Steps" style="max-height: 520px;">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">3 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 8.4: Phase 1 Steps -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="8">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 8</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Phase 1: Pre-deployment validation</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat(3, 1fr);">
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">1</div>
                        <h4>Understand Context</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Map your use case, identify stakeholders, recognize you're building for YOUR context</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">2</div>
                        <h4>Build Reference Dataset</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">10-20 examples of scenarios you cannot get wrong with clear expectations</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">3</div>
                        <h4>Implement Metrics</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Code-based for objective properties, LLM judges for subjective (with calibration)</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="mini-callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <p><span class="accent">Output:</span> Confidence that your system works as intended before real users interact</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">4 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 8.5: Phase 2 Steps -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="8">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 8</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Phase 2: Production monitoring</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap: 16px;">
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 20px;">
                        <div class="card-number">4</div>
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Log Filtering</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.85rem;">Priority + signal-based sampling</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 20px;">
                        <div class="card-number">5</div>
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Metric Selection</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.85rem;">Impact, reliability, cost analysis</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 20px;">
                        <div class="card-number">6</div>
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Guardrails + Flywheel</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.85rem;">Real-time critical + batch improvement</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="padding: 20px;">
                        <div class="card-number">7</div>
                        <h4 style="font-size: 1rem;">Issue Discovery</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.85rem;">Evolve framework as you learn</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <p><span class="accent">Output:</span> Smart filtering + cost-effective metrics + proactive detection + continuous improvement</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">5 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 8.6: Key Principles -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="8">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 8</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Key principles throughout</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="definitions" style="gap: 16px;">
                    <div class="def-box">
                        <h4>Start Simple</h4>
                        <p>Begin with basic approaches that provide clear value. Add complexity only when justified.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box">
                        <h4>Focus on Context</h4>
                        <p>Generic evaluation doesn't work. Everything must be tailored to your use case, users, and business.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box">
                        <h4>Embrace Evolution</h4>
                        <p>Your framework should continuously improve as you discover new failure modes.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box highlight">
                        <h4>Connect to Improvement</h4>
                        <p>The goal is better AI systems, not perfect measurement. Focus on actionable insights.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">6 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 8.7: What You End Up With -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="8">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 8</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>What you end up with</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <div class="hierarchy-list" style="gap: 12px;">
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 16px 20px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 2px;">Confidence before deployment</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Systematic validation that your system works as intended</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 16px 20px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 2px;">Effective production monitoring</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Smart filtering and evaluation that scales with your system</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 16px 20px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 2px;">Proactive issue detection</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Early warning systems that catch problems before they grow</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                        <div class="hierarchy-item accent" style="padding: 16px 20px;">
                            <div>
                                <h4 style="margin-bottom: 2px;">Continuous improvement</h4>
                                <p style="font-size: 0.9rem;">Feedback loops that help your system get better over time</p>
                            </div>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/evaluation_lifecycle.png" alt="Evaluation Lifecycle">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">7 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 8.8: Key Takeaways -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="8">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 8</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Key takeaways</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid">
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">01</div>
                        <h4>Two-phase approach</h4>
                        <p>Pre-deployment validation builds confidence. Production monitoring maintains quality.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">02</div>
                        <h4>Seven clear steps</h4>
                        <p>A roadmap connecting all concepts into actionable implementation.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">03</div>
                        <h4>Never complete</h4>
                        <p>Build for patterns you anticipate, discover patterns you couldn't predict.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout">
                    <p><span class="label">Up next →</span> Common misconceptions that trip up teams building AI systems</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">8 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Transition: Chapter 8 → Chapter 9 -->
        <div class="slide transition-slide" data-chapter="8">
            <div class="transition-content">
                <div class="from-label">You now understand</div>
                <div class="from-chapter">The complete evaluation process</div>
                <div class="transition-arrow">↓</div>
                <div class="to-label">Next up</div>
                <div class="to-chapter">Common misconceptions</div>
                <p class="transition-why">You have the complete framework. Before you go build, let's address the most common misconceptions that trip teams up when implementing AI evaluation.</p>
            </div>
        </div>

        <!-- ======================= -->
        <!-- CHAPTER 9: COMMON MISCONCEPTIONS -->
        <!-- ======================= -->

        <!-- Slide 9.1: Title -->
        <div class="slide title-slide" data-chapter="9">
            <div class="course-branding">
                <div class="course-title">AI Evals for Everyone</div>
                <div class="course-by">by LevelUp Academy</div>
            </div>
            <div class="chapter-label">Chapter Nine</div>
            <h1>Common <span class="highlight">Misconceptions</span></h1>
            <p class="subtitle">Clearing up the confusion about AI evaluation</p>
            <div class="slide-number">1 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 9.2: Foundation Misconceptions -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="9">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 9</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Foundation misconceptions</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="hierarchy-list" style="gap: 16px;">
                    <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 20px; border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <div>
                            <h4 style="margin-bottom: 4px; color: #ef4444;">"Benchmarks predict my product success"</h4>
                            <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">Model evaluations test general capabilities. Your product has unique requirements, constraints, and users.</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                    <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 20px; border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <div>
                            <h4 style="margin-bottom: 4px; color: #ef4444;">"Engineers can design evaluation alone"</h4>
                            <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">Effective evaluation requires collaboration: domain experts, product teams, AND engineers.</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                    <div class="hierarchy-item" style="padding: 20px; border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <div>
                            <h4 style="margin-bottom: 4px; color: #ef4444;">"Evaluation is a one-time setup"</h4>
                            <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">AI systems are non-deterministic, user behavior evolves, requirements change. Evaluation is continuous.</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">2 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 9.3: Pre-Deployment Misconceptions -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="9">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 9</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Pre-deployment misconceptions</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="two-col">
                    <div class="col" style="border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h3 style="color: #ef4444;">"I need comprehensive coverage from day one"</h3>
                        <p style="color: #737373;">Start small with 10-20 high-quality examples. Focus on quality over quantity.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="col" style="border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h3 style="color: #ef4444;">"Code metrics aren't sophisticated enough"</h3>
                        <p style="color: #737373;">Simple code checks are fast, reliable, cheap. Use them first before adding complexity.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="two-col" style="margin-top: 24px;">
                    <div class="col" style="border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h3 style="color: #ef4444;">"LLM judges are the best way"</h3>
                        <p style="color: #737373;">They're powerful but expensive, slow, and can be inconsistent without calibration.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="col" style="border-left: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h3 style="color: #ef4444;">"Detailed criteria = correct LLM judges"</h3>
                        <p style="color: #737373;">Calibration requires hundreds of examples and weeks of iteration, not just good prompts.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">3 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 9.4: Production Misconceptions -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="9">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 9</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Production misconceptions</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 20px;">
                    <div class="card" style="border-top: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4>"Evaluate every interaction"</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">Impossible at scale. Smart signal-based sampling is more effective.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="border-top: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4>"Need dozens of metrics"</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">More metrics = more noise. Better to have 3-5 that drive decisions.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="border-top: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4>"Online is always better"</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">Online must be fast. Complex analysis belongs in offline batch processing.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card" style="border-top: 3px solid #ef4444;">
                        <h4>"Metrics are fixed once set"</h4>
                        <p style="font-size: 0.95rem;">Metrics retire and update as you learn. User behavior evolves.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">4 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 9.5: Evals vs A/B Testing -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="9">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 9</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Misconception: evals vs A/B testing</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content">
                <div class="text-content">
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px; color: #ef4444; font-weight: 600;">"I need to pick one approach"</p>
                    <p style="margin-bottom: 24px;">This is a <span class="accent">false dichotomy</span>. They serve different purposes and work better together.</p>
                    <div class="two-col" style="gap: 24px;">
                        <div class="col" style="padding: 24px;">
                            <h3 style="font-size: 1rem;">Evaluation Metrics</h3>
                            <p style="color: #737373; font-size: 0.95rem;">Monitor known patterns and behaviors you understand</p>
                        </div>
                        <div class="col dark" style="padding: 24px;">
                            <h3 style="font-size: 1rem;">A/B Testing</h3>
                            <p style="color: #a3a3a3; font-size: 0.95rem;">Discover new patterns through explicit and implicit user signals</p>
                        </div>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="image-content">
                    <img src="images/misconceptions_evaluation_vs_ab_testing.png" alt="Evals vs A/B Testing">
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">5 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 9.6: Why Misconceptions Persist -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="9">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 9</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Why these misconceptions persist</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 24px;">
                    <div class="card">
                        <h4>AI evaluation is new</h4>
                        <p>Unlike traditional software testing, systematic AI evaluation is still emerging</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <h4>Complexity creates uncertainty</h4>
                        <p>Complex systems make simple approaches seem inadequate (even when they're not)</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <h4>Tool marketing influences thinking</h4>
                        <p>Vendors promote sophisticated solutions that may be overkill</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <h4>Success stories lack context</h4>
                        <p>Case studies don't include the failures and iterations that led to success</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">6 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 9.7: The Right Mindset -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="9">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 9</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>The right mindset</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="definitions" style="gap: 16px;">
                    <div class="def-box" style="background: #f0fdf4; border-color: #22c55e;">
                        <h4 style="color: #166534;">Start simple and evolve</h4>
                        <p style="color: #166534;">Begin with basic approaches that provide clear value</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box" style="background: #f0fdf4; border-color: #22c55e;">
                        <h4 style="color: #166534;">Focus on YOUR context</h4>
                        <p style="color: #166534;">Generic solutions rarely work. Tailor to your use case.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box" style="background: #f0fdf4; border-color: #22c55e;">
                        <h4 style="color: #166534;">Embrace collaboration</h4>
                        <p style="color: #166534;">Combine technical, domain, and business perspectives</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box" style="background: #f0fdf4; border-color: #22c55e;">
                        <h4 style="color: #166534;">Prioritize actionable insights</h4>
                        <p style="color: #166534;">Measure things that drive real improvements</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">7 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 9.8: Key Takeaways -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="9">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 9</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Key takeaways</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="cards-grid">
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">01</div>
                        <h4>Benchmarks ≠ Product success</h4>
                        <p>Model capabilities don't predict performance in your specific context.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">02</div>
                        <h4>Simple often wins</h4>
                        <p>Code-based metrics before LLM judges. Small datasets before comprehensive.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="card">
                        <div class="card-number">03</div>
                        <h4>Evolution is expected</h4>
                        <p>Metrics retire and update. Your framework should continuously improve.</p>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div class="callout">
                    <p><span class="label">Up next →</span> Glossary of terms to help you navigate evaluation vocabulary</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-number">8 / 8</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Transition: Chapter 9 → Chapter 10 -->
        <div class="slide transition-slide" data-chapter="9">
            <div class="transition-content">
                <div class="from-label">You now understand</div>
                <div class="from-chapter">What not to do</div>
                <div class="transition-arrow">↓</div>
                <div class="to-label">Finally</div>
                <div class="to-chapter">Reference glossary</div>
                <p class="transition-why">A quick reference guide to all the key terms and concepts we've covered, so you can speak the same language as your team.</p>
            </div>
        </div>

        <!-- ======================= -->
        <!-- CHAPTER 10: GLOSSARY OF TERMS -->
        <!-- ======================= -->

        <!-- Slide 10.1: Title -->
        <div class="slide title-slide" data-chapter="10">
            <div class="course-branding">
                <div class="course-title">AI Evals for Everyone</div>
                <div class="course-by">by LevelUp Academy</div>
            </div>
            <div class="chapter-label">Chapter Ten</div>
            <h1>Glossary of <span class="highlight">Terms</span></h1>
            <p class="subtitle">Making sense of the evaluation vocabulary</p>
            <div class="slide-number">1 / 7</div>
        </div>

        <!-- Slide 10.2: Core Evaluation Terms -->
        <div class="slide" data-chapter="10">
            <div class="chapter-marker">Chapter 10</div>
            <div class="slide-header">
                <h2>Core evaluation terms</h2>
            </div>
            <div class="slide-content stacked">
                <div class="definitions" style="gap: 16px;">
                    <div class="def-box">
                        <h4>Evaluation</h4>
                        <p>The overall process of assessing how an AI system behaves. Includes designing metrics, running tests, analyzing results.</p>
                    </div>
                    <div class="def-box">
